extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan attacked on the final Mur de Huy hairpins to become the first Belgian winner since Gilbert, beating VALVERDE who was led out by MAS.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint drove tempo on penultimate lap with 9km to go, neutralizing breakaway for team structure rather than individual result.
MAS Enric did full leadout for VALVERDE on final Mur climb, revealing Movistar prioritized legacy over youth opportunity.
KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren attacked between final two climbs with VAN SEVENANT, timing late enough to survive until Mur base.
BETTIOL Alberto set up CARR Simon from breakaway, showing EF willing to burn domestiques for low-probability breakaway gambles.

rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: positive: Won from reduced group on steepest Mur hairpins, suggesting superior explosive power on 18-19% gradients.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Required MAS leadout but still beaten by TEUNS, showing five-time winner now needs support to contend.
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Third place finish demonstrates Mur suits his power profile better than expected for grand tour climber.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: Fourth place without challenging suggests diminished punch on signature climb where he won three times previously.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Paced hard on penultimate lap for others rather than personal result, confirming pure domestique role.
rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: negative: Dropped on Côte de Cherave before finishing circuit, confirming ongoing form issues mentioned in commentary.
rider_signals.CARR Simon: positive: Survived to final lap from breakaway as climber, showing tactical value in future hilly one-day races.
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: Led final attack with VAN SEVENANT and held until Mur base, demonstrating improved classics timing.
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: positive: Fifth place suggests punchy climbing form that could transfer to similar Ardennes finishes.
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: Sixth place confirms consistent Mur performance despite lacking winning acceleration on steepest gradients.
